entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 03 - Declarative field control with @mandatory
Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 04 - Namespaces, contexts and name prefixes
Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 05 - Portable functions
Supporting data migration from other systems (SAP Solution Manager, etc.)
entry.content:
Integration of several products is always a challenging task, data accuracy and consistency are paramount. With this April 30th release, we are taking a significant step forward in strengthening that.  

It is all about the process models. We now offer a mechanism to verify their uniqueness across the products which are integrated, like SAP Signavio, SAP Cloud ALM, SAP Solution Manager and others. Process models are created in SAP Signavio, then are sent to and reside in other systems as well. For example, you can model a process in SAP Signavio and send it to SAP Cloud ALM and to SAP Solution Manager or any other system. Then the same process model can be sent from SAP Solution Manager or any other application to SAP Cloud ALM.  

If an SAP Signavio process model exists in SAP Cloud ALM, when syncing it to SAP Cloud ALM, SAP Signavio will update it in SAP Cloud ALM. If SAP Signavio process model does not exist in SAP Cloud ALM, when syncing it to SAP Cloud ALM, SAP Signavio will create it in SAP Cloud ALM. 

We preserve the uniqueness of process models across products by introducing external reference, which is provided by every product and verified each time when there are attempts from SAP Signavio to create or update a process model in SAP Cloud ALM. SAP Cloud ALM stores the external reference of every process model like a single point of truth for cross-product reference. 

As a user you don’t have to do anything: the additional cross-product uniqueness verification is there for you. 

For further reference please find more information on the SAP Help Portal: 

https://help.sap.com/docs/signavio-process-transformation-suite/9552dd8c5c384ea1bf9f6e27fe0dcfe7/0c070733ff0140e7bd10b531bb6d6b21.html 

https://help.sap.com/docs/signavio-process-manager/user-guide/exporting-bpmn-diagrams-revisions-in-sap-signavio-to-sap-cloud-alm  (in the Note)

entry.title: Why SAP S/4HANA Transformations Fail When Business Processes Are Automated Before They Are Optimized
entry.content:
Why SAP S/4HANA Transformations Fail When Business Processes Are Automated Before They Are Optimized

Introduction

One of the most common mistakes I have observed during SAP S/4HANA transformation programs is the tendency to automate existing business processes without first evaluating whether those processes are effective.

Organizations often invest significant time and resources implementing automation, workflows, robotic process automation (RPA), AI-powered assistants, and system integrations. However, if the underlying process is inefficient, inconsistent, or poorly designed, automation may simply accelerate existing problems rather than solve them.

Over the years, I have seen organizations achieve greater transformation success when they focus first on process optimization and then on automation.

The Automation Trap

Many transformation programs begin with the goal of reducing manual effort.

While this objective is understandable, teams sometimes assume that every manual activity should be automated.

Questions such as:

  • Can this approval be automated?

  • Can AI make this decision?

  • Can we eliminate manual intervention?

are frequently discussed.

However, a more important question is often overlooked:

Is this process designed correctly in the first place?

Automating an inefficient process rarely creates an efficient outcome.

When Automation Creates New Problems

In one transformation program, multiple approval layers had evolved over several years.

The process contained redundant approvals, overlapping responsibilities, and unnecessary delays.

Rather than simplifying the process, the organization initially focused on automating the approval workflow.

The result was predictable.

Approvals moved electronically instead of manually, but the overall cycle time remained largely unchanged because the process itself was still inefficient.

The organization eventually redesigned the approval structure, eliminated unnecessary steps, clarified ownership, and then automated the optimized workflow.

Only then did measurable improvements occur.

Process Optimization Before Automation

Before automation initiatives begin, organizations should evaluate:

Process Complexity

Does the process contain unnecessary steps?

Process Ownership

Is accountability clearly defined?

Business Value

Does each activity contribute meaningful value?

Standardization

Are different teams following the same process?

Compliance Requirements

Which controls are truly required?

Answering these questions often reveals opportunities to simplify operations before introducing automation.

The Role of SAP S/4HANA

SAP S/4HANA provides powerful capabilities for process standardization and automation.

However, the platform delivers the greatest value when organizations use the transformation as an opportunity to challenge existing practices.

Instead of asking:

"What should we automate?"

Organizations should first ask:

"What should we improve?"

The most successful transformations typically combine process redesign, organizational alignment, and technology modernization.

Where AI Fits Into the Equation

The growing adoption of AI introduces another layer of opportunity.

AI can:

  • Identify process bottlenecks

  • Detect inefficiencies

  • Predict delays

  • Recommend workflow improvements

  • Analyze user behavior patterns

However, AI is most effective when applied to well-defined and optimized business processes.

Organizations that deploy AI on top of inconsistent processes often struggle to achieve expected outcomes.

Signs That a Process Needs Optimization First

Several indicators suggest that optimization should occur before automation:

  • Frequent exceptions

  • Multiple approval layers

  • Duplicate data entry

  • Unclear ownership

  • High rework rates

  • Inconsistent execution across teams

  • Long cycle times

When these symptoms exist, process redesign should generally precede automation initiatives.

Lessons Learned

One lesson I have learned throughout SAP transformation programs is that technology is rarely the root cause of process inefficiency.

Most challenges originate from process design, governance, ownership, or organizational complexity.

Automation can amplify the effectiveness of a good process.

It can also amplify the inefficiencies of a poor one.

The difference lies in whether optimization occurs before automation.

Final Thoughts

SAP S/4HANA transformations provide organizations with a unique opportunity to rethink how work is performed.

Rather than simply digitizing existing processes, organizations should use transformation programs to simplify, standardize, and optimize operations before introducing automation.

In my experience, the most successful SAP programs are not those that automate the most processes. They are the ones that improve the right processes first and then apply automation where it creates meaningful business value.

Optimization before automation is often the difference between transformation and digitized inefficiency.

entry.title: Re: How to Implement SAP Business One projects successfully
entry.content:
Great breakdown on the implementation steps. Having run 20+ SMB go-lives in the Hyderabad market, I'd add one critical nuance: the go-live week is where Hyderabad SMBs lose the most money, and it's almost never a technical issue.

Three failure patterns I see repeatedly:

1. Master data validation is skipped under time pressure.
Teams migrate from Tally/legacy spreadsheets and the item master is "good enough" on cutover night. Three weeks later, inventory reports are useless because unit of measure conversions weren't standardized. The fix: run a parallel inventory count in the old system AND SAP B1 for 2 weeks before go-live. Any variance >2% stops go-live until resolved.

2. User training is treated as a checkbox.
SMB owners assume the consultant will "handle it." What actually happens: 3 PowerPoint slides, a rushed demo, and a team that can log in but can't process a goods receipt. The result: workarounds that corrupt data, followed by 6 months of cleanup. Better model: identify 2-3 power users per department in week 1. Train them deeply. They become your go-live support army.

3. No cutover rollback plan.
If something breaks on go-live night, what's the decision tree? Who decides to rollback vs. push through? This sounds obvious but almost no SMB implementation documents this. The result: 48 hours of chaos before the inevitable rollback — which costs more than if you'd planned for it.

The single best investment: A formal 3-day parallel run before go-live where the team processes every real transaction in BOTH systems simultaneously. Painful? Yes. Expensive? Yes. Cheaper than data recovery after a bad cutover? Far less.

What specific module are you struggling with? Happy to go deeper.

— Venkata Siva Reddy Polu, TEKROI
SAP B1 implementation for Hyderabad pharma and manufacturing SMBs
TekAI: AI-powered operations intelligence for SAP B1 users

entry.title: Re: Unable to Restrict Edit Functionality in Display Main...
entry.content:
Hello @sandeep_shankarampet,

I think the best starting point to figure out which catalog, IAM app, or authorization is granting Edit access is the app Display Authorization Trace.

  • Start a trace for the user with the affected role
  • Login as the user and open the app Display Maintenance Order
  • Then immediately deactivate the trace (to keep the amount of data in the trace manageable)
  • Then analyze the traces results with status "successful" and "filtered": since the edit access is granted, the relevant catalog, IAM app or restriction should be listed here.

If you would like to have some help with analysing the trace results, feel free to share them here.

I hope this helps.

Best regards,

Kerisha

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 05 - Portable functions
Re: Asset Revaluation - SAP Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello!

The posting record for revaluation of historical Acquisition and Production Costs (APC) debits the asset balance sheet account (APC) and credits a revaluation reserve account in equity. The accumulated depreciation adjustment is posted simultaneously debiting the revaluation adjustment account and crediting the accumulated depreciation account. You can set these postings using account determination groups.

For Portugal under SNC, this means account 5891 (Excedentes de Revalorização) should be configured directly as the credit account in Asset Accounting account determination for revaluation postings. There is no standard SAP clearing account step in between, the system posts straight to the equity account you configure.

Depreciation is automatically recalculated on the revalued asset base, surplus realisation to Retained Earnings is not automatic. After revaluation, the APC and depreciation have been adjusted accordingly; the planned depreciation amount for the remaining useful life is recalculated based on the new revalued carrying amount.

Under SNC, the portion of account 5891 that corresponds to the additional depreciation charged in each period should be progressively transferred to Retained Earnings (account 551x). SAP does not generate this transfer automatically. It must be posted manually via a periodic FI journal entry, typically at year-end, for the amount equal to: (depreciation based on revalued cost) minus (depreciation based on original cost).

Deferred taxes on asset revaluation are not automatically calculated or posted. SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition does include a Deferred Tax Processing framework (documented in the SAP Help Portal under Financial Closing), but this is designed for timing differences arising from revenue and expense recognition, not for deferred taxes arising from asset revaluation surplus under SNC. For the Portugal context, where a revaluation of a fixed asset creates a temporary difference between the tax base and the carrying amount the deferred tax liability must be posted manually.

There is no standard configuration in Public Edition that automates the calculation of revaluation-specific deferred taxes. SAP's deferred tax engine handles transactional temporary differences (e.g. provisions, accruals) but does not integrate with the Asset Accounting revaluation to post revaluation deferred tax automatically.

The recommended standard best practice for Portugal in Public Edition is:

  1.  Configure account determination to credit 5891 directly on revaluation posting. No clearing account needed.
  2.  Run revaluation via the Post Asset Revaluation app. The system posts the APC adjustment and accumulated depreciation offset in one document. From the next depreciation run, depreciation is automatically based on the revalued amount.
  3.  Year-end manual entries: Post two manual FI journal entries annually:

The progressive transfer from 5891 to Retained Earnings for the additional depreciation portion. And the partial reversal of the deferred tax liability corresponding to that year's realisation.

  1.  Deferred tax initial posting: At the time of revaluation, post the deferred tax liability manually (Dr 5891 ) for the full temporary difference. This reduces the net equity surplus shown in 5891 to its after-tax amount.

SAP References:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3e5fcf2c768746049b5627bd5a42f720/00aed0aaf4a4423d99e71a1fb938e9c9.html?locale=en-US&q=Post+Asset+Revaluation

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3e5fcf2c768746049b5627bd5a42f720/dc70b0b2798b4601a5c80bdbeca6e551.html?locale=en-US&state=PRODUCTION&version=2602.500&q=Manual%20Revaluation%20in%20Asset%20Accounting

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3e5fcf2c768746049b5627bd5a42f720/a6a3c960b0b94d9aabc3570099c6398b.html?locale=en-US&state=PRODUCTION&version=2602.500&q=Maintain%20Asset%20Revaluation%20Index

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/asset-accounting-asset-revaluation-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-2502/ba-p/14040197

https://community.sap.com/t5/financial-management-blog-posts-by-members/asset-impairment-asset-revaluation-configuration-sap-s4hana/ba-p/13923367

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-finance-asset-accounting-frequently-asked/ba-p/13726519

entry.title: Re: Clarification on Best Practices for Activating Additi...
entry.content:
Hello!

As of SAP S/4HANA Cloud scope items 5HG (Asset Accounting – Additional Depreciation Area) and 5KF (Asset Under Construction – Additional Depreciation Area) are available. The prerequisite is that scope items need to be selected and activated during initial scoping, can’t be added subsequently. Once the initial scoping is completed, it is not possible to set up a new depreciation area if master data and transaction data already exist. Scope item 5HG is a prerequisite for activating scope item 5KF. This means that you must activate 5HG before you can activate 5KF. If scope item 5HG was not activated at the time of original implementation, can’t be activated post-go-live.

The first action to take is to verify whether 5HG is already active in current system, even if it was scoped but not yet used. If it is already active, you have a viable path forward.

Best practice approach if 5HG is active for activating depreciation area 15 linked to ledger 2L post-go-live:

  1. Verify ledger 2L is correctly configured: use the local accounting principle for the non-leading ledger at company code level because by doing so, you get a tax depreciation area and you can also assign an alternative fiscal year variant. Confirm that ledger 2L has an accounting principle assigned at company code level, this is the prerequisite for depreciation area 15 to post to it. This is done via Assign Accounting Principle to Ledger and Company Codes.
  2. Activate the additional depreciation area 15. Assign it to ledger 2L with the appropriate posting indicator (typically "Posts in Realtime" for a tax area that needs to generate actual G/L postings, not just derived values).
  3.  Define account determination for depreciation area 15.
  4.  Define depreciation keys for tax depreciation.
  5.  Update asset master data. For all existing assets, the depreciation key, useful life, and start date for depreciation area 15 must be maintained individually in each asset master record.

Best Practice Approach if 5HG was not activated at initial scoping, the standard configuration path is closed. The options in this scenario are:

Option A:  Raise a SAP support incident requesting post-go-live activation

There is no documented self-service path to activate 5HG after go-live, but SAP support may be able to assess whether it can be enabled in your specific tenant on a case-by-case basis. This is not guaranteed but is worth exploring before considering alternatives.

Option B: Tax depreciation tracking outside SAP Asset Accounting. If the system can’t accommodate a new depreciation area, the tax depreciation calculation and reporting can be maintained outside the SAP Asset Accounting subledger using a spreadsheet or a dedicated fixed asset register reconciled to the SAP book values at period end. This is a workaround used when the system constraint can’t be resolved.

Option C: Manual G/L postings restricted to ledger 2L. You can create a manual adjustment posting in the Post General Journal Entries app by entering the relevant depreciation expense accounts and assigning the posting to Ledger Group 2L to restrict the impact to local GAAP or tax purposes only.  This approach posts the tax depreciation difference manually to ledger 2L as a period-end or year-end journal entry keeping the tax depreciation visible in the G/L ledger without requiring a formal Asset Accounting depreciation area. It is a workaround, not a replacement for a proper depreciation area.

 

There is no standard functionality in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to automatically replicate or copy historical depreciation data from depreciation area 1 to a newly activated tax depreciation area. When a new depreciation area is activated after assets already exist, the system does not back-calculate or copy historical posted values. The new area starts with zero values from the point of activation. Historical tax depreciation values must be loaded manually.

The available options are:

- Migration Cockpit (for historical opening balances): loading of fixed asset legacy data including values per depreciation area

- Manual entry via Manage Fixed Assets app: For smaller asset populations, the accumulated tax depreciation values can be entered manually per asset in the Manage Fixed Assets app once the depreciation area is activated.

 

SAP References:

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-finance-asset-accounting-frequently-asked/ba-p/13726519

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/asset-accounting-configuration-guide-for-sap-s-4hana-public-cloud-edition/ba-p/14134075

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-options-for-accounting-principles-and/ba-p/13569553

entry.title: Re: completion rule in sales quotation
entry.content:
Hello!

The detailed item category control configuration is not available in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. 

In SAP R/3 and S/4HANA on-premise, the Completion Rule is set in the item category (transaction VOV7) for the quotation item category (typically AGN). Setting it to B (item completed when full quantity is referenced) combined with the update document flow flag in copy control (VTAA) is what causes the quotation reference status to update from "Not Referenced" - "Partially Referenced" - "Fully Referenced" as sales orders are created against it.

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is pre-configured with standard copying control table entries, and can’t create additional table entries. The item category configuration available in Public Edition exposes only a limited set of fields, the Completion Rule and the Update Document Flow flag in copy control are among the fields not available for modification. The standard delivered behavior is that the quotation reference status remains "Not Referenced" regardless of how many sales orders are created against it.

There is no configuration-only fix available within the standard Public Edition, but are practical approaches that can mitigate the business impact:

Option A: Use the Manage Sales Quotations app and document flow for manual monitoring. The Manage Sales Quotations displays the document flow for each quotation, showing all subsequent sales orders created with reference to it. While the reference status field does not update automatically, the document flow is maintained  so a user can see how many orders have been created from a quotation and for what quantities.

Option B: Use validity dates on the quotation to limit exposure. Configure a quotation validity period so that once the valid-to date passes, the quotation can no longer be referenced for new sales orders. The system does enforce the validity date check during "Create with Reference." This does not solve the duplicate order problem within the validity period but limits the window of exposure.

Option C: Once Quotation 0020000135 has been converted to the sales order, manually apply a Reason for Rejection to the quotation item to prevent further sales orders from being created against it.

 

SAP References:

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/item-category-configuration/qaq-p/12818805

https://learning.sap.com/courses/implementing-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-sales-configuration/introducing-copy-control

https://learning.sap.com/courses/implementing-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-sales-fundamental-business-processes/understanding-sales-quotation-process_c189a9c0-aa19-4643-843b-69f9f56ad5de

SAP KBA 3269803 — Unable to Add New Copying Control for Sales and Billing Documents in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

SAP KBA 2963059 — How to Submit Feature Requests for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: Re: Quantity conversion management between PC/KG in sales...
entry.content:
Hello!

First issue: Decimal values in the UoM conversion ratio.

SAP's unit of measure conversion in the material master does not accept decimal values directly in the numerator/denominator fields. You can only enter whole numbers in the numerator and denominator fields. If 0.468 KG corresponds to 1 PC, you must enter integer multiples, for example 468 KG = 1000 PC. The system converts all other units into the base unit of measure.

So the conversion 1 PC = 0.540 KG must be expressed as 540 KG = 1000 PC (or reduced to 27 KG = 50 PC). This is not a limitation unique to Public Edition, it applies across all SAP systems. The workaround is always to scale up to whole number while maintaining the same ratio.

 

Second issue: the actual weight communicated by the warehouse differs from the material master ratio.

The material master holds a fixed, static conversion ratio. When the warehouse communicates that 10 PC actually weigh 5.4 KG (not the theoretical 5 KG), there is no standard mechanism in a basic setup to update the conversion on a per-delivery or per-batch basis.

Alternative options in standard Public Edition:

Option A: Express decimals as whole number ratios in material master. For the material master conversion, express the ratio as integers. For example, instead of 1 PC = 0.540 KG, maintain 1000 PC = 540 KG. This allows the system to correctly handle the theoretical conversion without the decimal error. However, this still does not solve the variable actual weight problem, it only solves the decimal entry issue for a fixed ratio.

Option B: Enter actual KG quantity manually on the delivery. In the outbound delivery, the quantity field in KG can be overridden manually to reflect the actual weight communicated by the warehouse. In the Manage Outbound Deliveries app, the delivery quantity in the alternative unit (KG) can be adjusted at the item level before goods issue. This is a manual step but is operationally viable for lower volumes. The goods issue will then post in the actual KG quantity, correctly reflecting the warehouse communication.

There is one additional configuration point worth checking. In the configuration activity Units of Measurement, the decimal places and decimal rounding settings for each unit of measure determine how quantities are displayed and rounded during conversion. If decimal places are set to 0 for KG, the system will round conversion results to whole numbers even when the underlying calculation produces decimals. Ensure that KG is configured with sufficient decimal places (at least 3) for both display and conversion. This will allow the system to correctly handle and display quantities like 5.400 KG without rounding to 5 KG.

entry.title: Re: Large Technical Tables /AIF/PERS_XML
entry.content:
/AIF/PERS_XML stores the raw XML payload of AIF messages.

The notes "Not applicable" usually just means the fix is already included in your current SP level. You can verify in your system whether transactions /AIF/MSG_DEL, /AIF/PERS_DEL, /AIF/XML_COMPRESS or report /AIF/MESSAGE_DELETION exist or not.

You can go to SE16 → /AIF/PERS_XML and check which Namespace/Interface owns the most rows, and how old the oldest records are.

Then choose to archive or delete the relevant interfaces' messages.

Better to archive (instead of delete), which reduce the table size while keeping data accessible for audit purposes if needed.

entry.title: SAP Build Work Zone + SAP Service Cloud V2: Creating a True Single Entry Point for Customer Service
entry.content:
Introduction

In today's enterprise landscape, employees often need to navigate multiple applications to complete a single business process. Service agents switch between CRM systems, ERP applications, workflow tools, and custom applications throughout the day, resulting in fragmented user experiences and reduced productivity.

The vision of a Unified Digital Workplace is simple: provide users with a single entry point where they can access all relevant applications, data, workflows, and insights based on their role.

SAP Build Work Zone has become the strategic digital workplace solution for SAP customers. Organizations already leverage Work Zone as a central access point for solutions such as SAP S/4HANA, SAP SuccessFactors, and custom SAP BTP applications.

Now, the same experience can be extended to customer-facing teams by integrating SAP Service Cloud Version 2 (V2) into SAP Build Work Zone, enabling service agents and managers to work from a single, personalized workspace.

In this blog, we will explore the business value, architecture, implementation approach, and a demo scenario showcasing how SAP Build Work Zone and SAP Service Cloud V2 can work together seamlessly.

A Short Story

Meet Prasad, a Customer Service Manager at a global manufacturing company running SAP's full suite.

Every morning, Prasad logs into SAP Build Work Zone. On her home page, she sees her personalized tiles: open production orders from S/4HANA, pending approvals from SuccessFactors, and her team's KPI dashboard. Everything is in one place.

But then her phone rings. A key customer is escalating a service ticket — a machine breakdown at their site. Prasad switches to a completely different browser tab, logs into SAP Service Cloud V2 separately, searches for the customer, finds the ticket, checks the service history, and tries to correlate it with the spare parts availability from S/4HANA — which means another tab, another login.

Three systems. Three tabs. One frustrated manager. One unhappy customer.

This is the problem SAP Build Work Zone + SAP Service Cloud V2 integration solves.

After the integration, Prasad's Work Zone home page now has a dedicated Service Operations section. The Service Cloud V2 app is embedded as a tile and a full sidebar integration. She can:

  • See real-time ticket queue directly from the Work Zone homepage
  • Open and update service tickets without leaving Work Zone
  • WorkZone can Receive intelligent notifications when ticket SLA thresholds are breached

Her morning is now seamless. Her customer gets faster resolution. Her team is more productive.

That is the power of a single entry point.

Key Benefits

True Single Entry Point for All Personas

Persona                                                             Before                                                                          After

Service Agent

2 systems (Work Zone + Service Cloud)

1 — Work Zone

Service Manager

3 systems (Work Zone + Service Cloud + S/4HANA)

1 — Work Zone

IT Admin

Manage 3 separate portals

Manage 1 unified portal

Executive

Multiple dashboards

1 personalized command center

Key Benefits

  • Unified User Experience:  Access SAP Service Cloud V2, SAP S/4HANA, SAP SuccessFactors, and custom applications through one portal.
  • Single Sign-On :Reduce authentication complexity and improve user productivity.
  • Role-Based Access: Deliver personalized content, applications, and workflows based on user roles.
  • Improved Productivity: Minimize context switching between applications and browser tabs.
  • Extensibility: Integrate custom SAPUI5 and SAP BTP applications alongside standard SAP solutions.

Why bring Service Cloud V2 into Work Zone

 

How to surface SAP Service Cloud V2 in Work Zone

  • Subscribe to SAP Build Work Zone, standard or advanced edition

In your BTP subaccount, go to Service Marketplace and subscribe to SAP Build Work Zone. Advanced Edition is required if you need Digital Workplace Service features (content federation, external solutions). Standard is sufficient for most SAP Cloud app integrations.

 
Navigation path: BTP Cockpit → Subaccount → Service Marketplace → SAP Build Work Zone → Create subscription → choose edition → Assign role collections Launchpad_Admin and Launchpad_User to relevant users.

  • Create a destination for SAP Service Cloud V2

Work Zone discovers external SaaS apps through BTP Destinations. SAP Service Cloud V2 exposes OData and REST APIs under its tenant URL. Create a destination with OAuth2 client credentials so Work Zone can fetch app descriptors and launch URLs securely.

Destination config (key fields)

Name: SCV2
Type: HTTP · URL: https://<your-tenant>.crm.cloud.sap
Auth: OAuth2ClientCredentials
Token Service URL: IAS token endpoint
Additional Properties: HTML5.DynamicDestination = true, sap-platform = cf

  • Add Service Cloud V2 as a content provider in Work Zone

Now tell Work Zone where to find those deployed apps. Open the Work Zone Site Manager and register Service Cloud V2 as a content provider. Point it at the HTML5 App Repository app and the SCV2 destination. Work Zone will fetch the CDM and expose the tiles to administrators.

Navigation path

Work Zone Site Manager → Channel Manager → Add → SAP BTP → Content Provider Name: SAP Service Cloud V2 → Runtime Destination: SCV2 → Design-Time Destination: SCV2 → Fetch content → Save.

  • Assign Service Cloud tiles to roles and groups

After fetching content, the Service Cloud V2 tiles appear in the Content Manager under "Available Content". Assign them to a role (e.g., ServiceAgent) and add that role to a launchpad group. 

Navigation path : Work Zone Content Manager → Add to Role → Create/select role ServiceAgent → Assign group Service Cloud Apps → Publish site.
 

See it in action — one launchpad, everything connected

To demonstrate the integration capabilities of SAP Build Work Zone, i created a simple demo showcasing how different applications can be brought together through a unified user experience.

In this video , users can observe the following:

  • Sales Cloud and Service Cloud V2 Integration
    SAP Build Work Zone provides direct access to Sales and Service Cloud V2, allowing users to launch these applications seamlessly from a single location.
  • Access to Business Objects
    Users can navigate to important business objects such as Accounts and Cases directly from Work Zone, reducing the need to switch between multiple systems.
  • Custom Application Integration
    Custom applications, including SAPUI5 applications deployed on SAP BTP, can be embedded within Work Zone and accessed alongside standard SAP applications.
  • Cross-Application Navigation
    The same custom application embedded in Work Zone can also be accessed from Service Cloud V2, enabling a connected and consistent user journey across platforms.

Why This Matters

By bringing together SAP applications and custom solutions, SAP Build Work Zone becomes a true single entry point for users. Whether users need to access Service Cloud V2, Sales Cloud, business data, or custom applications, everything is available through a unified digital workplace.

This approach simplifies navigation, improves productivity, and delivers a more seamless user experience by reducing the need to manage multiple entry points and disconnected systems.

Conclusion

Organizations often struggle with users navigating across multiple applications and portals. With SAP Build Work Zone, businesses can create a centralized experience that connects SAP solutions and custom applications under one roof. The result is a more intuitive, efficient, and user-friendly workspace that helps employees focus on their work rather than searching for the right application.

I have faced this issue multiple times where external configurations get overwritten across tenants during transport via CTMS, requiring manual reconfiguration sometime. entry.title: Deploying a Node.js App to SAP BTP Cloud Foundry Using GitHub Actions
entry.content:
This post walks through setting up a simple CI/CD pipeline using GitHub Actions that automatically deploys a Node.js app to SAP BTP Cloud Foundry on every push to main.

What You'll Need

  • An SAP BTP account with Cloud Foundry enabled and a space to deploy to
  • A GitHub account
  • Node.js and the CF CLI installed locally
  • A non-SSO username and password for your BTP account (used by the pipeline

The App

We'll deploy a minimal Node.js Express app with a simple Fiori-styled frontend. Nothing fancy — the point is the pipeline, not the app.

The project structure is:

btp-cicd-demo/
├── .github/
│   └── workflows/
│       └── deploy.yml
├── public/
│   └── index.html
├── server.js
├── package.json
├── manifest.yml
└── .gitignore

server.js

const express = require("express");
const path = require("path");

const app = express();
app.use(express.static(path.join(__dirname, "public")));

app.get("/", (req, res) => {
  res.sendFile(path.join(__dirname, "public", "index.html"));
});

const PORT = process.env.PORT || 3000;
app.listen(PORT, () => console.log(`Server running on port ${PORT}`));

package.json

{
  "name": "btp-cicd-demo",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "description": "BTP CI/CD Demo App",
  "main": "server.js",
  "scripts": {
    "start": "node server.js"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "express": "^5.2.1"
  }
}

manifest.yml

applications:
- name: btp-cicd-demo
  memory: 128M
  buildpacks:
    - nodejs_buildpack

public/index.html

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
  <head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8" />
    <title>BTP CI/CD Demo</title>
    <script
      id="sap-ui-bootstrap"
      src="https://openui5.hana.ondemand.com/resources/sap-ui-core.js"
      data-sap-ui-theme="sap_fiori_3"
      data-sap-ui-libs="sap.m"
      data-sap-ui-compatVersion="edge"
      data-sap-ui-preload="async"
    ></script>
    <script>
      sap.ui.getCore().attachInit(function () {
        sap.ui.require(["sap/m/App", "sap/m/Page", "sap/m/Text"], function (App, Page, Text) {
          new App({
            pages: [
              new Page({
                title: "BTP CI/CD Demo",
                content: [
                  new Text({ text: "Deployed via SAP CI/CD Service" })
                ]
              })
            ]
          }).placeAt("content");
        });
      });
    </script>
  </head>
  <body class="sapUiBody sapUiSizeCompact">
    <div id="content"></div>
  </body>
</html>

.gitignore

node_modules/
.env

Initialise the repo, commit everything, and push to GitHub.

The Pipeline

Create .github/workflows/deploy.yml:

name: Deploy to Cloud Foundry

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v3

      - name: Install CF CLI
        run: |
          curl -L "https://packages.cloudfoundry.org/stable?release=linux64-binary&version=v8&source=github" | tar -zx
          sudo mv cf8 /usr/local/bin/cf

      - name: Deploy to Cloud Foundry
        env:
          CF_API: https://api.cf.ap10.hana.ondemand.com
          CF_USERNAME: ${{ secrets.CF_USERNAME }}
          CF_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.CF_PASSWORD }}
          CF_ORG: <your-org>
          CF_SPACE: <your-space>
        run: |
          cf login -a $CF_API -u $CF_USERNAME -p $CF_PASSWORD -o "$CF_ORG" -s "$CF_SPACE"
          cf push btp-cicd-demo

Update CF_API with your region endpoint, and CF_ORG and CF_SPACE with your actual org and space names. These are visible when you run cf target.

GitHub Secrets

The pipeline uses two secrets to authenticate with Cloud Foundry without hardcoding credentials in the workflow file. In your GitHub repo go to Settings, then Secrets and variables, then Actions, and add two repository secrets:

CF_USERNAME — your BTP account email address CF_PASSWORD — your BTP account password

These are encrypted by GitHub and injected into the workflow at runtime. They never appear in logs or the codebase.

Note: this approach requires a non-SSO login. If your account authenticates via a corporate identity provider, you will need a technical user created by your BTP administrator for pipeline use.

Running the Pipeline

Push the workflow file to main:

git add .
git commit -m "Add CI/CD workflow"
git push

Go to the Actions tab in your GitHub repo. You should see the workflow trigger immediately. Click on it to watch the steps run — checkout, CF CLI install, login, and push.

If everything is configured correctly you'll see a green tick and your app will be live at your CF route.

Once deployed, loading the app URL in your browser should give you something like this:

To prove the pipeline works end to end, make a change to index.html — something obvious like updating the page title — commit and push:

git add .git commit -m "Update page title"git push

Go to the Actions tab in GitHub. You should see the workflow trigger immediately against your commit:

Once the pipeline completes, reload the app URL and you should see the change reflected straight away — no manual deployment needed:

Cleaning Up

To stop the app without deleting it:

cf stop btp-cicd-demo

To delete the app and its route entirely:

cf delete btp-cicd-demo -f -r

To disable the GitHub Actions pipeline so it stops triggering on push, go to the Actions tab in your GitHub repo, find the workflow, click the three dots menu and select Disable workflow.

 

What This Gives You

Every push to main now triggers an automatic deployment to Cloud Foundry. No manual cf push, no forgetting to deploy, no environment drift between what's in the repo and what's running.

It's a simple starting point. From here you can add a build step, run tests before deploying, deploy to multiple spaces (dev, test, prod) based on branch, or introduce the MTA build tool for more complex landscapes.

Full Repo: https://github.com/neilaspin/btp-cicd-demo

entry.title: Re: GBP vs USD currency total not matching with exchange...
entry.content:
When you sum the GBP amounts (–21,150.08) and the USD amounts (+185,929.08), then divide USD ÷ GBP, you get –8.79. This is not an exchange rate issue but it is simply the mathematical ratio of two totals that include mixed signs and different daily rates. Because the GBP total is negative and relatively small compared to the USD total, the ratio looks distorted.

Why this happens is because, SAP does not recalculate exchange rates at totals level. It just aggregates amounts. If you try to “back-calculate” an exchange rate from totals, you’ll often get nonsensical results, especially when some line items are debit, others credit and different exchange rates were used across days. Local currency balances are negative while group currency balances are positive.

Having said that the exchange rate should only be interpreted at line-item level. At totals level, you should not expect USD ÷ GBP to equal a meaningful exchange rate. Instead, totals are just the sum of translated amounts. If you need a consistent rate for reporting, you should use period-end revaluation (FAGL_FC_VAL) to restate balances at a single rate. Or report using group currency balances directly, without trying to derive a rate from totals.

entry.title: Re: Employee as BP
entry.content:
Yes you can create Business Partners with roles like Customer (FLCU01/FLCU00) or Contact Person (BUP001), and leave out the Employee role for now. Later when SuccessFactors/HR is connected, the integration framework (e.g., SAP Cloud Platform Integration or SAP Integration Suite) can map SuccessFactors employees to existing BP records, as long as the BP number is consistent and unique.

Use a dedicated BP grouping/number range for Sales Employees. This ensures they are distinguishable from customers/vendors and avoids conflicts later. Maintain unique identifiers (e.g., Personnel Number placeholder, External ID, or Employee ID from SuccessFactors once available). Store these in BP fields like Identification Numbers or External ID so they can be matched later.

entry.title: Re: Collision Handling between Absences and Attendances in SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking - 1H 202
entry.content:
Hi @Ranganath_K ,

If you do not want to trigger a retrospective calculation, you can create a new time type and assign it effective-dated on the time profile. Configure collision handling for this new time type. Old time type will be unimpacted. Alternatively, you can set the earliest recalculation date in Time Management Configuration object from the date from which you want to trigger a recalculation. However, this will impact all recalculations. I would prefer to create a new time type.

Kind Regards,

Priyanka

entry.title: Header Freight condition in Purchase order

In this case, the PO document currency is maintained as USD, and the freight condition type is entered in QAR currency. The requirement is to post separate invoices for the material supplier and the freight supplier, with each invoice adopting the respective currency maintained in the PO:

  • Material Supplier Invoice – USD
  • Freight Supplier Invoice – QAR

Could you please advise on the configuration settings and process steps required to achieve this scenario? Specifically, I would like to understand how SAP handles multiple currencies for planned delivery costs and whether any additional configuration is required to ensure that the supplier invoices are posted in their respective currencies.

Your guidance would be highly appreciated.

Best Regards

Dinesh

entry.title: Re: Employee as BP
entry.content: Yes, you can assign identical number ranges to the BP grouping and the related Customer/Supplier account groups for employees. The best practice is to use a dedicated internal number range for this grouping, so that the BP number remains consistent across all roles. This ensures that when the same employee acts as both Customer and Supplier, the account numbers align with the BP number. For future SuccessFactors integration, keep an identifier field reserved (e.g., External ID) to map employee records without creating duplicates.

entry.title: Re: Problem reinstalling SAP ASE
entry.content: If the previous installation wasn't fully removed, there might still be old ASE files, ports, or shared memory segments causing conflicts during the server build step. I've seen installs hang because of leftover configuration from an earlier instance. You could try checking whether any old ASE processes or resources are still present before reinstalling. Also, if you can share the specific error from the build phase, that would help narrow it down. By the way, I've run into similar issues with platform migrations on phonexa, where a partial cleanup caused the new deployment to get stuck unexpectedly.

entry.title: Re: Header Freight condition in Purchase order
entry.content: Yes, SAP supports this scenario. You need to configure freight condition types as planned delivery costs, assign them to the freight vendor, and allow currency entry in QAR. During invoice posting, the system will generate separate invoices in USD for the material supplier and QAR for the freight supplier, without additional currency customizing.

entry.title: Re: product Revision level
entry.content:
Dear @Noddy_R,

No, in Public Cloud, Product / Material Revision Level is not designed as an unrestricted free-text field comparable to the ECC External Revision Levels approach

SAP confirms two important points:

  1. The Change Number must be populated before the Create Revision Level function is available in Manage Product Master Data
  2. Revision levels are determined from the configuration activity / SSCUI Define Revision Levels for Materials

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3259773

Therefore, removing all maintained revision levels should not be used as a supported way to allow any revision level value. The clean-core Public Cloud approach is to define the required revision level values in configuration, for example P1 - P5 for preproduction and the required later numeric or alphanumeric revision codes

For the post-migration mass load the correct technical approach is to use API_PRODUCT with the Product Change Master relationship. SAP confirms that updating the material revision level with a change number via API_PRODUCT must be done using a $batch request to:

Product('product number')/_ProductChangeMaster

with the values Product, ChangeNumber and RevisionLevel in the payload

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3709249

Do not use API_CHANGEMASTER;v=0002 for this requirement. SAP confirms that revision level update through the Change Master API entity A_ChangeMstrObMgReMaterial is not supported, even if the API technically returns a successful 201 response

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2948975

If you receives the message Revision level cannot be created for material the Change Number setup should also be checked. SAP explains that this can happen when the Gen New flag is set for object type Material in the Change Master the resolution is to deselect this flag for the relevant Change Number

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2853498

Summary:

Deleting all revision levels is not a supported way to make revision levels unrestricted. In Public Cloud the safe approach is to maintain the allowed revision level values, assign them with a valid Change Number and use API_PRODUCT / Product Change Master with $batch for mass loading

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: How to Implement SAP Business One projects successfully
entry.content:
Solid overview — and having run 20+ SMB go-lives in the Hyderabad market, I'd add one critical nuance that most implementation guides skip: the go-live week is where Hyderabad SMBs lose the most money, and it's almost never a technical issue. It's a data readiness and visibility gap.

Here's what I see repeatedly:

1. Master data is "good enough" on cutover night — and wrong for months after.
Teams migrate from Tally or legacy spreadsheets and skip proper validation under time pressure. Three weeks later, inventory reports are unreliable because unit of measure conversions weren't standardised, or item master cost prices weren't updated. The fix: run a parallel inventory count in the old system AND SAP B1 for at least 2 weeks before go-live. Any variance >2% stops go-live until resolved. No exceptions.

2. Real-time operational visibility is treated as a post-go-live "nice to have."
This is the one that costs the most. Teams go live, start processing transactions, and only discover blind spots at month-end — when it's too late to course-correct. The smarter approach: define your key operational KPIs (inventory turnover, batch cost per unit, cashflow position, supplier quality flags) before go-live, and verify you can pull them on Day 1. If you can't see your numbers in real time, you're flying blind during the most critical window.

For pharma and manufacturing clients specifically, batch cost gap analysis has become a non-negotiable pre-go-live check — understanding what your system says a batch costs versus what it actually cost in the old system. Reconciling that gap before go-live prevents weeks of painful post-implementation cleanup. We've started using AI-assisted analysis (via TekAI, our SAP B1 operations layer) to surface these gaps automatically rather than running manual reconciliations. The time savings on cutover preparation alone is significant.

3. No rollback decision tree.
If something breaks on go-live night, who decides to push through vs. rollback? This sounds obvious but almost no SMB implementation documents this. The result: 48 hours of chaos. Document the decision criteria in advance — what conditions trigger a rollback, who has authority, and what the recovery path looks like.

The single best investment: A formal 3-day parallel run before go-live where the team processes every real transaction in BOTH systems simultaneously. Painful, yes. But far cheaper than data recovery after a bad cutover.

Happy to share our go-live checklist template for SAP B1 SMB implementations if useful.

— Venkata Siva Reddy Polu, TEKROI
SAP B1 implementation for Hyderabad pharma and manufacturing SMBs | TekAI: real-time operations intelligence on SAP B1

entry.title: Steps to Create Root Object in SAP CRM Web UI
entry.content:
In this Blog I have explained in detail on how to create Root Object in SAP CRM Web UI.

Steps:

  • Create a new Master Table name as ZMAST_DET_TEST3 using transaction SE11.
  • Create the Key Attribute Structure in SE11 for the above Table.
  • Create the Attribute Structure in SE11.
  • Create the Table Type for the above Attribute Structure In se11.
  • Create a API Class ZCL_DETAILS_API_3 with below methods:
      • CREATE_DETAILS_3
      • SAVE_DETAILS_3
      • GET_DETAILS_3
      • CHANGE_DETAILS_3
  • Add the Global Attribute GT_DETAILS_3 TYPE REF TO ZATTR_MAST3_TAB.
  • Create a Custom Genil Object class ZCL_DETAILS_MODEL and update the Super Class as CL_WCF_GENIL_ABSTR_COMPONENT and redefine the below Methods:
    • IF_GENIL_APPL_ALTERNATIVE_DSIL~SAVE_OBJECTS
    • IF_GENIL_APPL_INTLAY~CREATE_OBJECTS
    • IF_GENIL_APPL_INTLAY~GET_OBJECTS
    • IF_GENIL_APPL_INTLAY~MODIFY_OBJECTS
  • Create the Root Object in TCode GENIL_MODEL_BROWSER
  • Make the entry in View Cluster CRMVC_GIL_APPDEF in TCode sm34

Detailed Explanation:

Create a new Master Table name as ZMAST_DET_TEST3 using transaction SE11.

Create the Key Attribute Structure in SE11 for the above Table

Create the Attribute Structure in SE11

Create the Table Type for the above Attribute Structure In se11:

Create a API Class ZCL_DETAILS_API_3 with below methods:

  • CREATE_DETAILS_3
  • SAVE_DETAILS_3
  • GET_DETAILS_3
  • CHANGE_DETAILS_3

 

 

 

 

 

Add the Global Attribute GT_DETAILS_3 TYPE REF TO ZATTR_MAST3_TAB.

Method SAVE_DETAILS_3:

 

 

 

 

 

 

Method GET_DETAILS_3:

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Method CHANGE_DETAILS_3:

 

 

 

Create a Custom Genil Object class ZCL_DETAILS_MODEL and update the Super Class as CL_WCF_GENIL_ABSTR_COMPONENT and redefine the below Methods:

  • IF_GENIL_APPL_ALTERNATIVE_DSIL~SAVE_OBJECTS
  • IF_GENIL_APPL_INTLAY~CREATE_OBJECTS
  • IF_GENIL_APPL_INTLAY~GET_OBJECTS
  • IF_GENIL_APPL_INTLAY~MODIFY_OBJECTS

entry.title: Re: We would like to modify the PDF layout generated from...
entry.content:
Hi Olivia,

To clarify the technical behavior of the system: when you use the standard "Export as" button and choose PDF directly from the Fiori table toolbar in the Manage Customer Line Items app, SAP does not use an underlying PDF template.

This button triggers a generic system export function that is designed to generate flat data. While it retains your active filters and basic sorting, it systematically ignores UI-level visual grouping or subtotals. Because there is no Adobe Form or template linked to this specific button, it cannot be modified via Key User Extensibility. See Sap Note 2652146 

Regarding your suggestion to build a custom CDS View: building a custom report alone will not solve this specific PDF layout issue, as the standard export button in a custom report will still flatten the data in the exact same way. See suggestion number 3.

To achieve your goal, I see three approaches, ranked from least to most effort:

1. The Standard Workaround (No-Code): Instead of exporting to PDF, export the flat list to a Spreadsheet (Excel). You can then quickly apply Excel's native Subtotal function or a Pivot Table to group the data by Customer ID and calculate the summaries. Finally, "Save as PDF" directly from Excel. While this adds a manual step, it requires zero development effort, and once your Excel is fine-tuned, it does not take so much time to generate such a report.

2. Use SAP Output Control (Correspondence): If a pre-formatted PDF directly from the system is strictly required, do not use the UI export button. Instead, trigger a standard Correspondence (such as an Open Items List). This process does use SAP Output Control and an underlying Adobe Form template (.xdp). You can download the standard template via the Maintain Form Templates app, apply your specific grouping and subtotal logic within Adobe LiveCycle Designer, and upload it as a custom form.

3. Full Custom Application: If you proceed with the custom CDS View route, you would need to build a Custom Fiori Application and explicitly integrate it with SAP Output Management (setting up BRF+ rules) to trigger a custom Adobe Form. This represents significantly more development and maintenance effort compared to the options above.

Hope it helps,

Michal

entry.title: Re: Steps to Create Root Object in SAP CRM Web UI
entry.content:
method CREATE_DETAILS_3. *************************************************************************************

* In this Method we update the details to be Updated into the Database Table *************************************************************************************

DATA : lv_data3 like line OF GT_DETAILS_3.

* First we lock the table

CALL FUNCTION 'ENQUEUE_EZMAST_DET_TEST3'

EXPORTING MODE_ZMAST_DET_TEST3 = 'E'

EXCEPTIONS

FOREIGN_LOCK = 1

SYSTEM_FAILURE = 2

OTHERS = 3 .

IF sy-subrc <> 0.

* Implement suitable error handling here

ENDIF.

* Assign the various values

lv_data3-guid = is_det_key_3-guid.

lv_data3-ind_number = is_det_key_3-IND_NUMBER.

lv_data3-new1 = 'C'.

* Appending to the Internal Table

Append lv_data3 to gt_details_3.

rv_success = 'X'.

endmethod.

entry.title: Re: Steps to Create Root Object in SAP CRM Web UI
entry.content:
method SAVE_DETAILS_3.
****************************************************************************
* In this method we Save the details to the Database
****************************************************************************
DATA : wa_det_3 TYPE ZMAST_DET_TEST3.
DATA : lv_success TYPE abap_bool.

FIELD-SYMBOLS: <details_attr_n> LIKE LINE OF gt_details_3.

lv_success = 'X'.
* We read the Interal table to get the values
READ TABLE gt_details_3 ASSIGNING <details_attr_n> WITH KEY guid = det_key_3-guid
                                                                                                           IND_NUMBER = det_key_3-ind_number.
* Here we check the value of sy-syubrc
CHECK sy-subrc = 0.
* Here we check if the Attribute is Create or Modify
CASE <details_attr_n>-new1.
WHEN 'C' OR 'M'.
* Move the values
MOVE-CORRESPONDING <details_attr_n> TO wa_det_3.
* Update the Database Table
MODIFY ZMAST_DET_TEST3 FROM wa_det_3.
WHEN OTHERS.
ENDCASE.

CALL FUNCTION 'DEQUEUE_EZMAST_DET_TEST3'
EXPORTING
MODE_ZMAST_DET_TEST3 = 'E'
guid                                             = det_key_3-guid
IND_NUMBER                          = det_key_3-IND_NUMBER.
* Clearing the Structure
CLEAR det_key_3.
endmethod.

entry.title: Re: Steps to Create Root Object in SAP CRM Web UI
entry.content:
method GET_DETAILS_3.
**************************************************************************
* In this method we get the details
**************************************************************************
FIELD-SYMBOLS <data> LIKE LINE OF gt_details_3.
* Checking if the structure is not initial
IF is_det_key_3 IS NOT INITIAL.

* We read from Buffer.
READ TABLE gt_details_3 WITH KEY  guid                = is_det_key_3-guid
                                                                       ind_number = is_det_key_3-ind_number
                                            ASSIGNING <data>.
ELSE.
READ TABLE gt_details_3 WITH KEY new1 = 'C' ASSIGNING <data>.

IF sy-subrc <> 0.
RETURN.
ENDIF.
ENDIF.

IF sy-subrc = 0.
IF <data>-new1 EQ 'C' OR <data>-new1 EQ 'M'.
* We move the values to the structure
MOVE-CORRESPONDING <data> TO es_det_attr_3.
RETURN.
ENDIF.
ENDIF.
endmethod.

entry.title: Re: Steps to Create Root Object in SAP CRM Web UI
entry.content:
method CHANGE_DETAILS_3.
*******************************************************************
* In this method we Change the details
*******************************************************************
* Data Declarations
FIELD-SYMBOLS : <old> TYPE simple,
<new> TYPE simple,
<line> TYPE ZATTR_MAST3,
<name> TYPE name_komp.
* Read the internal table
READ TABLE gt_details_3 WITH KEY guid = is_det_attr_3-guid
                                                                      ind_number = is_det_attr_3-ind_number  ASSIGNING <line> .

* Check the value ofsy-subrc
CHECK sy-subrc IS INITIAL.
* Looping at the Internal table
LOOP AT it_names ASSIGNING <name>.
ASSIGN COMPONENT <name> OF STRUCTURE <line> TO <old>.
CHECK sy-subrc = 0.
ASSIGN COMPONENT <name> OF STRUCTURE is_det_attr_3 TO <new>.
* Checking the value of sy-subrc
CHECK sy-subrc = 0.
<old> = <new>.
ENDLOOP.
<line>-new1 = 'M'.
rv_success ='X'.
endmethod.

entry.title: Re: Steps to Create Root Object in SAP CRM Web UI
entry.content:
method IF_GENIL_APPL_ALTERNATIVE_DSIL~SAVE_OBJECTS.
******************************************************************
* In this method we Save the Objects to the Database and is triggered when the user clicks *'Save' on the UI
******************************************************************
DATA lv_det_key_3 TYPE ZATTR_MAST_DET_TEST_KEY3.
FIELD-SYMBOLS <lo_object> TYPE crmt_genil_obj_inst_line.
* Here we loop at the Internal table
LOOP AT ct_object_list ASSIGNING <lo_object>.
* We check for the Object Instance.
CASE <lo_object>-object_name.
WHEN 'Details3'.
TRY.
CALL METHOD cl_crm_genil_container_tools=>get_key_from_object_id(
EXPORTING
iv_object_name = <lo_object>-object_name
iv_object_id = <lo_object>-object_id
IMPORTING
es_key = lv_det_key_3 ).
CATCH cx_crm_genil_general_error .
ENDTRY.
* Here we call the Custom API to Save the data.
CALL METHOD zcl_details_api_3=>save_details_3
CHANGING
det_key_3 = lv_det_key_3.
* Check if the variable is Initial
IF lv_det_key_3 IS INITIAL.
<lo_object>-success = abap_true.
ENDIF.
ENDCASE.
ENDLOOP.
endmethod.

entry.title: Re: Steps to Create Root Object in SAP CRM Web UI
entry.content:
method IF_GENIL_APPL_INTLAY~CREATE_OBJECTS.
*************************************************************************************
* Here we instantiate new business objects at runtime in the GenIL buffer
*************************************************************************************
TYPES : BEGIN OF ty_det3,
IND_NUMBER TYPE numc5,
END OF ty_det3.

DATA : lit_det3     TYPE TABLE OF ty_det3.
DATA : lv_key3     TYPE ZATTR_MAST_DET_TEST_KEY3,
            lv_guid3    TYPE crmt_object_guid,
           lv_success TYPE char1,
           wa3              TYPE ty_det3,
            lv_count     TYPE i VALUE 0.

* Get the Name Pairs table and fill the structure
fill_struct_from_nvp_tab( EXPORTING it_parameters = it_parameters
                                                  CHANGING  cs_parameter = lv_key3 ).
* Here we Create the Guid.
CALL FUNCTION 'GUID_CREATE'
IMPORTING
ev_guid_16 = lv_guid3.

* Here we create the data for the New Entry into the table
SELECT IND_NUMBER FROM ZMAST_DET_TEST3 INTO TABLE lit_det3.
* Sort the Internal table
SORT lit_det3 DESCENDING.
* Checking if the Internal table is not initial
IF lit_det3 IS NOT INITIAL.
* Here we loop at the Internal table
LOOP AT lit_det3 INTO wa3.
* Here we Increment the count by 1
   lv_count = wa3-IND_NUMBER + 1.
EXIT.
ENDLOOP.
ELSE.
lv_count = 1.
ENDIF.
* Here we assign the values
lv_key3-guid = lv_guid3.
lv_key3-IND_NUMBER = lv_count.

* Here we call the API Class fills the the global attribute.
CALL METHOD zcl_details_api_3=>create_details_3
EXPORTING
is_det_key_3 = lv_key3
IMPORTING
rv_success = lv_success.

* Here we add the object to Data Container
IF lv_success IS NOT INITIAL.
iv_root_list->add_object( iv_object_name = iv_object_name
is_object_key = lv_key3 ).
ENDIF.
endmethod.

entry.title: Re: Lack of History Tracking for Key Figure Changes in Ob...
entry.content:
Hi, correct. This is working as designed. 

In reporting the business wants to see the data as of now. Not any deltas, or versioning. In that case you can create snapshots as versions. Alternative is to create snapshots only for changed measures. But that will be more difficult to reconcile, but if you use DSP just as EDI, then this is possible. Another method would be CDC. 

Even in Planning Scenarios, the Versions are kind of snapshots. 

Depends on the reason / root cause why do you need to version the measures. I would suggest snapshots. This way you are always sure to have a fully independent version for all measures, attributes, ... 

Martin

entry.title: Re: Steps to Create Root Object in SAP CRM Web UI
entry.content:
method IF_GENIL_APPL_INTLAY~GET_OBJECTS.
*************************************************************************
* Triggered by the UI layer to read and fetch the attributes of specific
* Business Object Layer (BOL) entities
*************************************************************************
DATA : lv_key3        TYPE ZATTR_MAST_DET_TEST_KEY3,
             lv_det_att3 TYPE ZATTR_MAST3,
             lv_root          TYPE REF TO if_genil_cont_root_object.
* Here we get the first object.
lv_root = iv_root_list->get_first( ).
* Here we get the Key
lv_root->get_key( IMPORTING es_key = lv_key3 ).
IF lv_root->check_attr_requested( ) = abap_true.
* Here we call the Custom API class to get the attributes.
zcl_details_api_3=>get_details_3( EXPORTING is_det_key_3 = lv_key3
IMPORTING es_det_attr_3 = lv_det_att3 ).

IF lv_det_att3 IS NOT INITIAL.
* Set the attributes
lv_root->set_attributes( lv_det_att3 ).
lv_root = iv_root_list->get_next( ).
ENDIF.
ENDIF.
endmethod.

entry.title: Re: Steps to Create Root Object in SAP CRM Web UI
entry.content:
method IF_GENIL_APPL_INTLAY~MODIFY_OBJECTS.
************************************************************************
* This method is invoked whenever a user creates, deletes, or changes
* attribute values on the UI
************************************************************************
DATA : lv_root                           TYPE REF TO if_genil_container_object,
             lv_props                       TYPE REF TO if_genil_obj_attr_properties,
             lv_changed_objects  TYPE                crmt_genil_obj_instance,
             lv_det_attr3                 TYPE ZATTR_MAST3,
             lt_changed_attr          TYPE crmt_attr_name_tab,
             lv_det_key3                 TYPE ZATTR_MAST_DET_TEST_KEY3,
             lv_success                    TYPE abap_bool,
             lv_change                      TYPE crmt_genil_attr_property.

CHECK iv_root_list IS BOUND.
* Here we get the first object from Container.
lv_root = iv_root_list->get_first( ).
IF lv_root->get_delta_flag( ) IS NOT INITIAL.
* Here we get name of the object.
CASE lv_root->get_name( ).
WHEN 'Details3'.
* Here we get the Attributes Property Object
lv_props = lv_root->get_attr_props_obj( ).
CALL METHOD lv_props->get_name_tab_4_property
EXPORTING
iv_property = if_genil_obj_attr_properties=>modified
IMPORTING
et_names = lt_changed_attr.
* Here we get the values
lv_root->get_key( IMPORTING es_key = lv_det_key3 ).
lv_root->get_attributes( IMPORTING es_attributes = lv_det_attr3 ).
* Here we move the values
MOVE-CORRESPONDING lv_det_key3 TO lv_det_attr3.
* Here we call the API to change the details
CALL METHOD zcl_details_api_3=>CHANGE_DETAILS_3
EXPORTING
is_det_attr_3 = lv_det_attr3
it_names = lt_changed_attr
IMPORTING
rv_success = lv_success.

IF lv_success IS NOT INITIAL.
lv_changed_objects-object_name = 'Details3'.
lv_changed_objects-object_id = cl_crm_genil_container_tools=>build_object_id( lv_det_key3 ).
APPEND lv_changed_objects TO et_changed_objects.
ENDIF.
WHEN OTHERS.
ENDCASE.
ENDIF.
endmethod.
